title: Anthropic says US directive forced it to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers
friendlyTitle: Anthropic’s newest models were suddenly switched off
summary: Anthropic said a US export-control directive required suspension of access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals, leading the company to disable both models for all customers while it works on compliance and restoration.
friendlySummary: Anthropic says a US government order forced it to pause access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The company turned them off for everyone while it sorts out compliance.
whyItMatters: This is a concrete example of frontier-model access becoming a policy and operations issue, not just a product launch. Teams building on top-tier models need fallback plans and vendor-diversity strategies when access can change abruptly.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If your work depends on a single AI model, this is a reminder to keep backups. Model access can now change because of regulation, not just technical outages.
